title: 〜ごとに
description: 〜ごとに attaches to a noun to mean "every ~" or "at each ~" — marking regular intervals or that something applies to each member of a group.

content.text: 〜ごとに (from 毎（ごと）) attaches directly to a noun, often a number plus a counter, to mean "every ~" at regular intervals. The に is often kept but can be dropped in casual speech.

content.japanese: 会社（かいしゃ）は２年（ねん）ごとに新（あたら）しいパソコンをくれます。
content.english: The company gives us a new computer every two years.

content.japanese: 一日（いちにち）四時間（よじかん）ごとに散歩（さんぽ）に行（い）きます。
content.english: I go for a walk every four hours during the day.

content.japanese: オリンピックは四年（よねん）ごとに開（ひら）かれます。
content.english: The Olympics are held every four years.

content.japanese: この薬（くすり）は六時間（ろくじかん）ごとに飲（の）んでください。
content.english: Take this medicine every six hours.

content.text: With a countable noun, ごとに can also mean "each" or "one by one" — the statement applies to every individual member.

content.japanese: この電車（でんしゃ）は駅（えき）ごとに止（と）まります。
content.english: This train stops at every station.

content.japanese: 人（ひと）ごとに考（かんが）え方（かた）が違（ちが）います。

content.text: 〜ごとに (every X, at each X) overlaps with 〜おきに (with X in between). 一日（いちにち）ごとに means "every day," but 一日（いちにち）おきに means "every other day." For intervals, ごとに includes the counted unit; おきに leaves a gap of that size between events.

examples.japanese: 会社（かいしゃ）は２年（ねん）ごとに新（あたら）しいパソコンをくれます。
examples.english: The company gives us a new computer every two years.

examples.japanese: 一日（いちにち）四時間（よじかん）ごとに散歩（さんぽ）に行（い）きます。
examples.english: I go for a walk every four hours during the day.

examples.japanese: オリンピックは四年（よねん）ごとに開（ひら）かれます。
examples.english: The Olympics are held every four years.

examples.japanese: この薬（くすり）は六時間（ろくじかん）ごとに飲（の）んでください。
examples.english: Take this medicine every six hours.

examples.japanese: この電車（でんしゃ）は駅（えき）ごとに止（と）まります。
examples.english: This train stops at every station.

examples.japanese: 人（ひと）ごとに考（かんが）え方（かた）が違（ちが）います。
